nodes.label: Patent Development Cooperation Agreement
nodes.content:
PATENT DEVELOPMENT COOPERATION AGREEMENT
This Patent Development Cooperation Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into on 1 March 2026 (the "Effective Date") by and between:
Party A:
Aurora Motion Technologies Ltd.
Registered Address: 17/F, Meridian Innovation Tower, 88 Linhai Road, Nanshan District, Shenzhen, Guangdong, PRC
Authorized Representative: Ms. Zhao Min
Email: legal@auroramotion.example

Party B:
Vertex Consumer Products Co., Ltd.
Registered Address: 9/F, East Harbor Industrial Centre, 216 Xingyuan Avenue, Bao'an District, Shenzhen, Guangdong, PRC
Authorized Representative: Mr. Huang Jiarui
Email: ip@vertexcp.example
Main Contract Name and Number:
Product Development and Supply Framework Agreement No. AM-VCP-2026-0318
Product Model and Product Name Involved:
VX-S1 Modular Smart Grip Device
Patent Application Number Involved:
CN-PA-2026-AX774291 (internal reference only; formal filing number to be updated upon official acceptance)
Party A and Party B are each a "Party" and together the "Parties."
To maintain a stable long-term cooperative relationship and protect the lawful interests of both Parties, the Parties hereby agree as follows after friendly consultation.

nodes.label: 1. Definitions
nodes.content: 1. Definitions

nodes.label: 1.1 Main Contract
nodes.content:
1.1 Main Contract
"Main Contract" means the Product Development and Supply Framework Agreement identified above, together with any written amendments signed by both Parties.

nodes.label: 1.2 Cooperation Results
nodes.content:
1.2 Cooperation Results
"Cooperation Results" means any technical solution, design optimization, structural improvement, process refinement, drawing, prototype, sample, test output, or other patentable achievement generated during the performance of the Main Contract.

nodes.label: 1.3 Patent Application Right
nodes.content:
1.3 Patent Application Right
"Patent Application Right" means the right to apply for a patent before the competent authority in accordance with applicable law.

nodes.label: 1.4 Patent Right
nodes.content:
1.4 Patent Right
"Patent Right" means the exclusive legal right granted by the competent authority after patent approval, including invention patents, utility model patents, and design patents.

nodes.label: 2. Scope and Purpose
nodes.content: 2. Scope and Purpose

nodes.label: 2.1 Purpose
nodes.content:
2.1 Purpose
This Agreement governs the ownership, filing, exploitation, transfer, licensing, and protection of patent-related rights arising from the Cooperation Results generated during the term of the Main Contract.

nodes.label: 2.2 Relationship with Main Contract
nodes.content:
2.2 Relationship with Main Contract
This Agreement is supplemental to the Main Contract. In the event of inconsistency between this Agreement and the Main Contract on patent ownership, exploitation, or filing responsibility, this Agreement shall prevail, unless the Parties expressly agree otherwise in writing under Section 8.4.

nodes.label: 3. Ownership of Patent Application Right and Patent Right
nodes.content: 3. Ownership of Patent Application Right and Patent Right

nodes.label: 3.1 Sole Ownership by Party A
nodes.content:
3.1 Sole Ownership by Party A
The Patent Application Right and Patent Right shall belong solely to Party A in any of the following circumstances: the concept arises from joint discussion, but Party A independently completes the substantive technical development and produces the principal Technical Carrier.

nodes.label: 3.2 Sole Ownership by Party B
nodes.content:
3.2 Sole Ownership by Party B
The Patent Application Right and Patent Right shall belong solely to Party B where Party B independently provides the complete technical solution and corresponding drawings, and Party A has not materially contributed to technical improvement, structural modification, or preparation of the Technical Carrier.

nodes.label: 3.3 Joint Ownership
nodes.content:
3.3 Joint Ownership
The Patent Application Right and Patent Right shall be jointly owned by the Parties where: Party B provides a substantially developed 3D design or structural drawing, and Party A materially revises, engineers, or implements the same into a completed Technical Carrier.

nodes.label: 3.4 Presumption in Case of Evidentiary Conflict
nodes.content:
3.4 Presumption in Case of Evidentiary Conflict
If the Parties dispute whether a Cooperation Result falls under Sections 3.1, 3.2, or 3.3, the initial ownership status shall be presumed to be joint ownership until the Parties reach written agreement or a final determination is made under Section 8.3.

nodes.label: 4. Filing Procedure and Cooperation Timeline
nodes.content: 4. Filing Procedure and Cooperation Timeline

nodes.label: 4.1 Disclosure of Potentially Patentable Results
nodes.content:
4.1 Disclosure of Potentially Patentable Results
Each Party may, at its discretion, inform the other Party of any Cooperation Result that it considers potentially suitable for patent filing at a time it deems appropriate. If one Party does not raise any written objection within ten (10) business days after receiving such information, the receiving Party shall be deemed to have accepted the disclosing Party's description of the Cooperation Result, including the proposed inventiveness, ownership position, and filing arrangement. Where a Party fails to disclose a Cooperation Result in a timely manner, the other Party may determine, based on its own understanding of the project, whether such result should be filed and in whose name the filing should proceed.

nodes.label: 4.2 Preliminary Review Period
nodes.content:
4.2 Preliminary Review Period
Following any disclosure under Section 4.1, the Parties may conduct an internal review of the relevant technical materials within fifteen (15) business days, or within such longer period as may be reasonably required by either Party. During such review, Party A shall have the primary authority to assess inventiveness, commercial relevance, filing necessity, and provisional ownership under Section 3, and Party B shall cooperate with such assessment. If the Parties do not reach agreement during the review period, Party A's preliminary determination shall govern the filing strategy and interim ownership treatment unless and until otherwise agreed in writing.

nodes.label: 4.3 Key Timeline Milestones
nodes.content:
4.3 Key Timeline Milestones
For contract administration purposes, the Parties acknowledge the following illustrative timeline:
identification of patentable result
written disclosure
preliminary ownership review
filing preparation
patent submission
office action response
grant, rejection, withdrawal, or abandonment
commercialization or licensing
infringement enforcement, invalidation defense, or expiry
This timeline is interpretive and operational only and does not independently create rights beyond those expressly stated in this Agreement.

nodes.label: 5. Exploitation, Licensing, and Transfer
nodes.content: 5. Exploitation, Licensing, and Transfer

nodes.label: 5.1 Solely Owned Patents
nodes.content:
5.1 Solely Owned Patents
Where a Patent Right is solely owned by one Party under Section 3.1 or 3.2, the owning Party shall have the exclusive right to exploit, license, assign, pledge, or otherwise dispose of that Patent Right, subject to any confidentiality or commercial restrictions under the Main Contract.

nodes.label: 5.2 Jointly Owned Patents
nodes.content:
5.2 Jointly Owned Patents
Wher a Patent Right is jointly owned under Section 3.3, either Party may, in light of commercial needs, assign, license, sublicense, commercialize, or otherwise dispose of all or part of such Patent Right to a third party upon prior notice to the other Party. If the other Party does not object in writing within five (5) business days after receipt of such notice, it shall be deemed to have consented. In urgent commercial circumstances, a Party may proceed first and complete internal confirmation with the other Party afterward.

nodes.label: 5.3 Internal Exploitation by a Co-owner
nodes.content:
5.3 Internal Exploitation by a Co-owner
Unless otherwise agreed in writing, each co-owner of a jointly owned Patent Right may independently use, exploit, implement, adapt, commercialize, or authorize others to use the relevant patent technology in connection with its own business operations. For the avoidance of doubt, manufacturing through affiliates, subcontractors, distributors, customers, or other commercial partners engaged by a co-owner shall be deemed part of such co-owner's own business operations and shall not be regarded as a transfer, assignment, or third-party license requiring separate consent under Section 5.2.

nodes.label: 5.4 Commercial Buyout Option
6. Patent Validity, Infringement, and Risk Allocation
nodes.content: 6. Patent Validity, Infringement, and Risk Allocation

nodes.label: 6.1 Duty to Preserve Validity
nodes.content:
6.1 Duty to Preserve Validity
The patent owner, or both Parties in the case of joint ownership, shall act in good faith to preserve the validity of the patent and shall not knowingly take action that materially jeopardizes patentability, enforceability, or legal status.

nodes.label: 6.2 Infringement by Third Parties
nodes.content:
6.2 Infringement by Third Parties
If either Party becomes aware of suspected infringement of a Patent Right governed by this Agreement, that Party shall promptly notify the other Party. In the case of joint ownership, the Parties shall consult on enforcement strategy, cost allocation, and settlement approach.

nodes.label: 7. Breach, Remedies, and Termination
nodes.content: 7. Breach, Remedies, and Termination

nodes.label: 7.1 Material Breach
7.1(a) Unauthorized Assignment or Transfer
nodes.content: (a) assigns, licenses, discloses, or transfers a patent right in violation of Section 5.2;

nodes.label: 7.1(b) Refusal to Cooperate in Filing
nodes.content: (b) refuses without reasonable cause to cooperate in filing under Section 4.4;

nodes.label: 7.1(c) Concealment of Inventorship Evidence
nodes.content: (c) intentionally conceals inventorship or contribution evidence relevant to ownership determination under Section 3; or

nodes.label: 7.1(d) Material Breach of Main Contract IP Obligations
nodes.content: (d) materially breaches any confidentiality, exclusivity, or IP cooperation obligation under the Main Contract in a manner that affects patent ownership or exploitation.

nodes.label: 7.2 Damages
nodes.content:
7.2 Damages
The breaching Party shall compensate the non-breaching Party for all losses directly arising from the breach, including reasonable investigation costs, filing losses, re-filing costs, legal fees, enforcement costs, and reasonably foreseeable business losses. If the actual loss is difficult to calculate, the breaching Party shall pay liquidated damages of USD 500,000, subject to adjustment by the competent adjudicating authority if required by applicable law.

nodes.label: 7.3 Right to Terminate
nodes.content:
7.3 Right to Terminate
If either Party believes that the other Party has committed or may commit a material breach under Section 7.1, it may suspend performance, withhold cooperation, or terminate this Agreement at any time upon written notice or other reasonable form of communication. The breaching Party may, where practicable, be given an opportunity to cure within fifteen (15) days, but failure to provide such opportunity shall not affect the validity of termination where the non-breaching Party considers immediate termination commercially necessary. Where the relevant breach affects, or may affect, the Main Contract, the non-breaching Party may also terminate, suspend, or renegotiate all or any part of the Main Contract in its discretion, notwithstanding any inconsistent procedure or limitation in the Main Contract.

nodes.label: 7.4 Accrued Rights
nodes.content:
7.4 Accrued Rights
Termination of this Agreement shall not affect any rights, interests, remedies, commercial expectations, ownership claims, payment claims, pending discussions, or obligations that either Party considers accrued, intended to survive, or reasonably connected with the Cooperation Results or the Main Contract, whether or not expressly stated in this Agreement. Any clause that may by its nature continue after termination shall remain binding for such period as either Party reasonably requires to protect its interests.

nodes.label: 8. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
nodes.content:
8. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the People's Republic of China and, where relevant, by generally accepted international commercial practices. Any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall first be resolved through friendly consultation. If consultation is unsuccessful, either Party may submit the dispute to the competent people's court of Nanshan District, Shenzhen, to arbitration in another jurisdiction acceptable to the submitting Party, or to any other dispute resolution forum that such Party considers appropriate in light of the circumstances. Pending final resolution of the dispute, each Party may continue to exercise any contractual or proprietary rights it deems necessary to preserve its commercial or legal position.

nodes.label: Signatures
nodes.content:
SIGNATURES

Name: Zhao Min
Date: 1 March 2026
